subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statement: The company has decided to reduce its workforce to cut operational costs.
Assumptions:
I. Reducing workforce will significantly reduce operational costs.
II. Remaining employees will be able to handle the workload efficiently.

options.text: Only I is implicit
Only II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit

solution.final_answer: Both I and II are implicit

explanation.concept: An assumption is an unstated premise necessary for the conclusion or action in the statement to hold true.
step_by_step: Step 1: Identify the action — reducing workforce to cut costs.
Step 2: Check if cost reduction depends on workforce reduction (Assumption I). Yes, necessary.
Step 3: Check if work continuity depends on remaining employees (Assumption II). Yes, also necessary.

why_correct.A: Incorrect as it ignores operational feasibility after workforce reduction.
why_correct.B: Incorrect as cost reduction must also depend on workforce reduction.
why_correct.C: Both are necessary for decision validity.

why_incorrect.D: Incorrect because both assumptions support the decision logically.

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statement: The government has imposed higher taxes on luxury goods to increase revenue.
Assumptions:
I. Demand for luxury goods will not decrease significantly due to higher taxes.
II. People purchasing luxury goods can afford higher taxes.

options.text: Only I is implicit
Only II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit

solution.final_answer: Both I and II are implicit

explanation.concept: Assumptions ensure that the intended outcome of a decision is achievable under given conditions.
step_by_step: Step 1: Objective is increasing revenue.
Step 2: If demand drops significantly, revenue won't increase (Assumption I needed).
Step 3: Buyers must afford higher prices to sustain purchases (Assumption II needed).

why_correct.C: Both assumptions are essential for policy success.

why_incorrect.D: Contradicts the logical basis of policy.

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statement: Schools should introduce coding classes from early grades.
Assumptions:
I. Early exposure to coding improves problem-solving skills.
II. Students in early grades can understand coding concepts.

options.text: Only I is implicit
Only II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit

solution.final_answer: Both I and II are implicit

explanation.concept: A recommendation implies belief in both feasibility and benefit.
step_by_step: Step 1: Recommendation is introducing coding early.
Step 2: Benefit assumption → improves skills (I).
Step 3: Feasibility assumption → students can learn it (II).

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statement: The company launched a new product despite declining market demand.
Assumptions:
I. The company expects the demand to increase in the future.
II. The product is unique enough to create its own demand.

options.text: Only I is implicit
Only II is implicit
Either I or II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit

solution.final_answer: Either I or II is implicit

explanation.concept: When a decision contradicts current trends, it must rely on at least one justification.
step_by_step: Step 1: Demand is declining, yet product launched.
Step 2: Either future demand will rise OR product will create demand.
Step 3: At least one assumption must hold, not necessarily both.

why_correct.C: One of the assumptions must justify the decision.

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statement: People are advised to exercise regularly to maintain good health.
Assumptions:
I. Exercise contributes to good health.
II. People are currently not exercising enough.

options.text: Only I is implicit
Only II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit

explanation.concept: Advice implies belief in effectiveness, not necessarily current behavior.
step_by_step: Step 1: Advice given → exercise improves health (I needed).
Step 2: Whether people currently exercise is not necessary (II optional).

why_incorrect.D: Incorrect as advice must be based on effectiveness.

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statement: The city has increased parking fees to reduce traffic congestion.
Assumptions:
I. Higher parking fees will discourage people from using personal vehicles.
II. Public transport is available as an alternative.

options.text: Only I is implicit
Only II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit

solution.final_answer: Both I and II are implicit

explanation.concept: A policy action assumes behavioral change and viable alternatives.
step_by_step: Step 1: Aim is reducing traffic.
Step 2: Discouragement requires higher fees to impact behavior (I).
Step 3: Alternative transport must exist (II).

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statement: The company has stopped advertising its top-selling product.
Assumptions:
I. The product no longer requires promotion.
II. Advertising does not significantly impact sales of this product.

options.text: Only I is implicit
Only II is implicit
Either I or II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit

solution.final_answer: Either I or II is implicit

explanation.concept: A decision to stop an action implies justification via redundancy or ineffectiveness.
step_by_step: Step 1: Advertising stopped.
Step 2: Either it is unnecessary OR ineffective.
Step 3: Only one justification is required.

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statement: Students are advised to revise regularly before exams.
Assumptions:
I. Revision improves retention.
II. Students forget what they study.

options.text: Only I is implicit
Only II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit

explanation.concept: Advice is based on effectiveness, not necessarily on current deficiencies.
step_by_step: Step 1: Advice given → must be beneficial.
Step 2: Revision improves retention (I required).
Step 3: Forgetting is not necessary assumption.

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statement: The bank has reduced interest rates to encourage borrowing.
Assumptions:
I. Lower interest rates make borrowing more attractive.
II. People are willing to take loans if interest rates are low.

options.text: Only I is implicit
Only II is implicit
Both I and II are implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit

solution.final_answer: Both I and II are implicit

explanation.concept: A strategy to influence behavior assumes both incentive effectiveness and response.
step_by_step: Step 1: Objective is to increase borrowing.
Step 2: Lower rates must attract borrowers (I).
Step 3: People must respond positively (II).

subject: Logical Reasoning
question_text:
Statement: The organization replaced manual processes with automation to improve efficiency.
Assumptions:
I. Automation reduces human error.
II. Manual processes were inefficient.
III. Automation is cost-effective in the long run.

options.text: Only I and II are implicit
Only II and III are implicit
Only I and III are implicit
All I, II, and III are implicit

solution.final_answer: All I, II, and III are implicit

explanation.concept: Complex decisions rely on multiple assumptions: problem existence, solution effectiveness, and feasibility.
step_by_step: Step 1: Inefficiency must exist (II).
Step 2: Automation must improve accuracy (I).
Step 3: It must be economically viable (III).

why_correct.D: All are required to justify replacement.
